name: nodekit
description: Server-side only. Node.js application toolkit providing config loading, structured pino logging, OpenTelemetry tracing, graceful shutdown, and hierarchical request context (AppContext).
when_to_use: Server-side only
avoid: Not for browser/frontend code — server-side Node.js only. Not for simple scripts that do not need logging or tracing infrastructure.

name: NodeKit Overview
description: NodeKit is a server-side Node.js toolkit that provides a structured foundation for building applications and scripts. It handles application configuration (file-based + env vars), structured logging via pino, OpenTelemetry tracing, Clickhouse telemetry, graceful shutdown signals, and a hierarchical request context system.
when_to_use: When building a Node.js service that needs structured logging, distributed tracing, and config management
As the base layer for ExpressKit-based web services
When you need a hierarchical request context with log scoping and abort signal propagation
avoid: In browser/frontend code — this is server-side only
For simple scripts that don't need logging or tracing infrastructure
content:
Initialize with `new NodeKit(options?)`. Options include disableDotEnv, configsPath (directory for JSON config files), and a config object to merge. The instance exposes:
- nodekit.config: merged AppConfig from file configs + env vars + options
- nodekit.ctx: root AppContext for the application
- nodekit.utils: redactSensitiveKeys, redactSensitiveHeaders, redactSensitiveQueryParams, isTrueEnvValue
- nodekit.addShutdownHandler(fn): register async cleanup on SIGTERM/SIGINT
- nodekit.setupDynamicConfig(namespace, setup): start polling for dynamic config

Environment variables: APP_NAME, APP_VERSION, APP_ENV, APP_INSTALLATION, APP_DEV_MODE, APP_LOGGING_LEVEL all map to AppConfig fields.

name: NodeKit
description: The main NodeKit class that bootstraps a Node.js application with config loading, logging, tracing, telemetry, and a root AppContext. Reads dotenv, merges file configs with env vars, initializes pino logger, sets up OpenTelemetry if enabled, and registers SIGTERM/SIGINT shutdown handlers.
when_to_use: As the entry point for any NodeKit-based application
avoid: Multiple instances per process — one NodeKit instance per application is the intended pattern
signature: new NodeKit(options?: { disableDotEnv?: boolean; configsPath?: string; config?: AppConfig }): NodeKit

name: AppContext
description: A hierarchical request/operation context class that carries config, logger, utils, and tracing spans. Supports child context creation, scoped logging with automatic prefix/postfix, OpenTelemetry span management, abort signal propagation, and key-value parameter storage with inheritable/non-inheritable semantics.
when_to_use: For scoped logging in request handlers — each request gets a child context
When distributed tracing spans need to be created and linked
To store per-request metadata (requestId, userId) that propagates to child contexts
avoid: Sharing one context across concurrent requests — each request must create its own child context
signature: new AppContext(name: string, params: ContextInitialParams | ContextParentParams): AppContext

name: AppError
description: A structured error class extending the native Error with optional code, details, and debug fields. Provides AppError.isAppError(e) for type-narrowing and AppError.wrap(error, args) to convert unknown errors into AppError instances while preserving the original stack trace.
when_to_use: Throwing structured errors from service or controller code that need machine-readable codes
Wrapping unknown errors from external calls with AppError.wrap() to preserve context
Type-narrowing in catch blocks with AppError.isAppError()
avoid: Using for HTTP-specific errors — set code to an HTTP status and let the error handler format the response
Throwing AppError without a message — always provide a human-readable message
signature: class AppError<T = {}> extends Error { code?: string | number; details?: T; debug?: object; static isAppError(error: unknown): error is AppError; static wrap(error: unknown, args?: AppErrorWrapArgs): AppError; constructor(message?: string, args?: AppErrorArgs<T>) }

name: AppContext API
description: Documents the key methods of AppContext used for request lifecycle management and structured logging in NodeKit applications.
when_to_use: When implementing request handlers that need scoped logging and context propagation
avoid: Calling end() or fail() multiple times on the same context
content:
Key methods:
- ctx.log(message, extra?): structured info log with prefix
- ctx.logError(message, error?, extra?): error log, sets span error status
- ctx.logWarn(message, error?, extra?): warn log
- ctx.create(name, params?): create a child context
- ctx.call(name, fn, params?): create child context, run fn, auto-end on completion
- ctx.end(): mark context as done, end tracing span
- ctx.fail(error?): log error, abort, end span
- ctx.set(key, value, {inheritable}): store param on context (inheritable by default)
- ctx.get(key): retrieve stored param
- ctx.isEnded(): check if context has ended
- ctx.getTime(): elapsed ms since context creation
- ctx.abortSignal: AbortSignal that aborts when ctx.end() is called
- ctx.getMetadata(): extract traceparent headers for outgoing requests

name: initTracing
description: Initializes OpenTelemetry distributed tracing for a NodeKit application. Reads tracing configuration from AppConfig (collector endpoint, protocol HTTP/gRPC, sampler, instrumentations) and starts the OpenTelemetry Node SDK. Supports W3C TraceContext, Baggage, and Jaeger propagation formats.
when_to_use: Called automatically by NodeKit when appTracingCollectorEndpoint is set in AppConfig
When manually configuring tracing before the NodeKit instance starts
avoid: Calling multiple times per process — initTracing starts the SDK and should be called once
Calling after the first request is handled — tracing must be initialized before any spans are created
signature: function initTracing(config: AppConfig, logger: NodeKitLogger): { sdk: NodeSDK; tracingSpanExporter: SpanExporter }

name: NodeKitLogger
description: The logger interface used throughout NodeKit. Defines info, error, warn, trace, debug methods that each accept either a plain message string or an (extra: Dict | undefined, message: string) overload for structured logging. The default implementation is PinoLogger backed by the pino library.
when_to_use: Type-hinting custom logger implementations that plug into NodeKit
Accepting a logger parameter in utilities that need to log without depending on pino directly
avoid: Using PinoLogger directly — depend on NodeKitLogger interface for testability
In request handlers — use ctx.log() / ctx.logError() from AppContext instead
signature: interface NodeKitLogger { info(message: string): void; info(extra: Dict | undefined, message: string): void; error(...): void; warn(...): void; trace(...): void; debug(...): void; }

name: REQUEST_ID_HEADER
description: The HTTP header name used by NodeKit to propagate request IDs between services. Value: `'x-request-id'`. Used when reading or writing the request ID header in incoming/outgoing HTTP calls.
when_to_use: Reading or setting the x-request-id header in HTTP clients or middleware
Forwarding the request ID to downstream services
avoid: Hardcoding 'x-request-id' — use this constant to stay consistent with NodeKit

name: REQUEST_ID_PARAM_NAME
description: The AppContext parameter key used to store and retrieve the request ID within a context. Value: `'requestId'`. Use ctx.get(REQUEST_ID_PARAM_NAME) to read the request ID from the current context.
when_to_use: Reading the request ID from ctx with ctx.get(REQUEST_ID_PARAM_NAME) in service or middleware code
Setting the request ID on ctx with ctx.set(REQUEST_ID_PARAM_NAME, id)
avoid: Hardcoding 'requestId' — use this constant for consistent access
